title: The Boilers Act, 2025
full_text: The Boilers Act, 2025 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: A2025-12 Act Number: 12 Enactment Date: 2025-04-04 Act Year: 2025 Short Title: The Boilers Act, 2025 Long Title: An Act to provide for the regulation of boilers, safety of life and property of persons from the danger of explosions of steam-boilers and for uniformity in registration and inspection during manufacture, erection and use of boilers in the country and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Commerce and Industry Department: Department of Promotion Of Industry and Internal Trade Enforcement Date: 01-05-2025 TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Boilers Act, 2025 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II CENTRAL BOILERS BOARD CHAPTER III INSPECTION, CERTIFICATION AND REGISTRATION CHAPTER IV APPEAL CHAPTER V OFFENCES AND PENALTIES CHAPTER VI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, commencement and application. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Central Boilers Board. Section 4. Technical Adviser. Section 5. Chief Inspector, Deputy Chief Inspector and Inspector. Section 6. Competent authority to grant welders certificate. Section 7. Conditions precedent for manufacture of boiler and boiler components. Section 8. Inspection during manufacture. Section 9. Inspection during erection. Section 10. Conditions precedent for repairing boiler and boiler components. Section 11. Prohibition of use of unregistered or uncertified boiler. Section 12. Registration. Section 13. Renewal of certificate. Section 14. Provisional order. Section 15. Use of boiler pending grant of certificate. Section 16. Revocation of certificate or provisional order. Section 17. Alteration and renewal of boiler. Section 18. Alteration and renewal of steam-pipe or boiler components. Section 19. Duty of owner at examination. Section 20. Production of certificate and provisional order. Section 21. Transfer of certificate and provisional order. Section 22. Powers of entry of Inspector. Section 23. Report of accident. Section 24. Appeal to Chief Inspector. Section 25. Appeal to Central Government. Section 26. Application for revision of order. Section 27. Minor penalties. Section 28. Penalties for illegal use of boiler. Section 29. Punishment for certain offences. Section 30. Penalty for tampering with register mark. Section 31. Penalty for breach of rules or regulations. Section 32. Recovery of penalties. Section 33. Limitation and previous sanction for prosecution. Section 34. Trial of offences. Section 35. Adjudication of penalties. Section 36. Appeal against orders of adjudicating officer. Section 37. Power of Central Government to give directions. Section 38. Exemptions. Section 39. Power of Central Government to make rules. Section 40. Power of Board to make regulations. Section 41. Rules and regulations to be laid before Parliament. Section 42. Power of State Government to make rules. Section 43. Finality of orders. Section 44. Power to remove difficulties. Section 45. Repeal and savings.
